_meta.description: Parking scenario overlay for Greenwood Residency (sid=100). Provides registered vehicles, slot allocations, violation type catalog, and DETERMINISTIC repeat-offender plates for testing the 5th-major-incident heuristic + clamping authorization flow.
design_notes: Parking is a complaint category WITHIN Greenwood, not a separate tenant. The seeder inserts vehicles into `vehicles` (society_id=100), allocates slots, and produces historical complaints with category='Parking Management' linked via vehicle_plate.

All vehicle plates use the Indian format STATE-DISTRICT-LETTERS-NUMBERS (e.g. MH-12-AB-1234). Plates are clearly synthetic — the LETTERS field uses 'ZZ' or 'XX' or 'ZX' in the repeat-offender plates so a human reading the dashboard can spot them at a glance.
All owner phones use the reserved test-phone range +9199000XXXXX per Part 0-B Layer 2. Clamping notifications will short-circuit at the Twilio chokepoint and log TEST_PHONE_SKIPPED.

basement_A.allocation_rule: one slot per occupied unit, allocated by unit-purchase order

visitor._note: Visitor slots are first-come; not allocated to any unit.

ev_charging.rules: EV vehicles only; ICE vehicle parked here = ev_lane_misuse violation

fire_lane.rules: any vehicle stationary here = fire_lane violation, immediate clamping eligibility

handicap.rules: non-permit holder parked here = hp_lane_misuse violation

violation_types.description: Vehicle blocks tower exit ramp or emergency egress
Parked in another resident's allocated slot
Parked outside marked slots in driveway / lobby area
Visitor vehicle exceeded 4-hour limit in visitor slot
Non-resident vehicle parked overnight without registration
Vehicle in marked fire lane — immediate clamping authorized
Persistent horn use / loud music in parking area

registered_vehicles_to_seed._note: Counts only — actual plate generation happens in Part 2 from synthetic-people deterministic seed. Plates use MH-XX-{LL}-{NNNN} format where district = 12 (Pune Borivali) and LL randomized.
registered_vehicles_to_seed.distribution: one registered vehicle per ~25 of 50 seeded units (i.e. ~half of seeded units have 1 vehicle, ~quarter have 2 vehicles)

deterministic_repeat_offender_plates._note: Three plates deliberately seeded with ≥3 violations in the past 30 days each — exercises the 5th-major-incident heuristic + clamping authorization flow. These are FIXED plates (not random) so testers can verify the same scenario each reseed.

violations_seeded._note: ★ 3rd violation in 30 days → 5th-major-incident heuristic should auto-flag

plates.expected_system_behavior: should appear in 'repeat offenders' admin view; resolved status means the cron will NOT re-escalate or re-notify

violations_seeded._note: Fire lane = auto-clamp-eligible; clamping was authorized historically

plates.expected_system_behavior: demonstrates the clamping-authorization audit trail; clamped=1 in vehicles table
demonstrates VISITOR-class repeat offender; owner is not a resident; system should still trigger 5th-major-incident flag

live_test_plates._note: These plates are RESERVED for the tester to use during live testing. The tester logs in, files a fresh parking complaint for one of these plates, and verifies the system's REAL-TIME behavior (not historical seeded data). Cron WILL fire for these.

plates_reserved_for_live_testing.purpose: Single-violation happy-path test (file complaint → owner notified → resolved → rated)
Repeat-offender escalation test (file 3 complaints in quick succession → verify 5th-major-incident trigger fires)
Fire-lane auto-clamp test (file fire_lane violation → verify immediate clamping-authorized state)
